records.ety: [OE. surfet, OF. surfait, sorfait, excess, arrogance, crime, fr. surfaire, sorfaire, to augment, exaggerate, F. surfaire to overcharge; sur over + faire to make, do, L. facere. See Sur-, and Fact.]

quotes: Let not Sir Surfeit sit at thy board.
Now comes the sick hour that his surfeit made.

defs: Fullness and oppression of the system, occasioned often by excessive eating and drinking.
quotes: To prevent surfeit and other diseases that are incident to those that heat their blood by travels.
Matter and argument have been supplied abundantly, and even to surfeit.

defs: To load the stomach with food, so that sickness or uneasiness ensues; to eat to excess.
quotes: They are as sick that surfeit with too much as they that starve with nothing.

defs: To indulge to satiety in any gratification.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Surfeited; p. pr. & vb. n. Surfeiting.]

defs: To feed so as to oppress the stomach and derange the function of the system; to overfeed, and produce satiety, sickness, or uneasiness; -- often reflexive; as, to surfeit one's self with sweets.
To fill to satiety and disgust; to cloy; as, he surfeits us with compliments.
